title: Walking in the Spirit-Filled Life
intro: Knowing who you are in Christ is the foundation. Walking in the Spirit is how you live from it. Identity without daily dependence becomes theology without transformation. The Spirit-filled life is not a destination — it is a walk, taken step by step, every day.
reading:
Gavin's outline addresses how the believer now lives — not from the old self but from the new, not in the flesh but in the Spirit.

Romans 6:6-11 calls us to consider ourselves dead to sin and alive to God in Christ Jesus. This is not a feeling to wait for. It is a truth to reckon with — to count as real and live from accordingly. Galatians 2:20 puts it in the most personal terms: I have been crucified with Christ. It is no longer I who live, but Christ who lives in me. The life I now live in the flesh I live by faith in the Son of God.

Galatians 5:16 gives the great promise wrapped inside the great command: walk by the Spirit, and you will not gratify the desires of the flesh. The promise is not that you will never feel the pull of the flesh — it is that the Spirit's power is greater, and walking in Him is how you live above what once controlled you.

Romans 8:14 ties the Spirit-filled life directly to sonship: all who are led by the Spirit of God are sons of God. To be led by the Spirit is not an occasional experience — it is the normal mode of existence for a child of God.

2 Corinthians 5:20 gives us our ambassadorial calling: we are ambassadors for Christ, God making His appeal through us. Every interaction is an opportunity to make God's appeal visible in the world.

Galatians 5:25 closes the picture: if we live by the Spirit, let us also keep in step with the Spirit. Not running ahead of Him. Not lagging behind. Step by step. In step.
reflection: Where in your daily life is it hardest to keep in step with the Spirit — which relationships, environments, or patterns pull you out of that rhythm most consistently?
abide: Holy Spirit, lead me today. Not just in the grand decisions but in the small ones — the words I choose, the attitudes I carry, the way I treat the people in front of me. I want to walk in step with You, moment by moment.
